A is not correct but R is correct
Both A and R are correct and R is NOT the correct explanation of A
solution: Beryllium has the smallest atomic size amongst the alkaline earth metals. The loss of electrons or oxidation is difficult. Beryllium has the least oxidation potential. As Beryllium has the least oxidation potential, it has the highest reduction potential(Less negative value of reduction potential). However, it's reducing nature is due to large hydration energy associated with the small size of Be2+ ion and relatively large value of the atomisation enthalpy of metal.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The Solvay process is also called ammonia recovery.

The calcium hydroxide is reacted with the ammonium chloride separated out of the carbonating tower by filtration.

Ca(OH)2(aq)+2NH4Cl(aq)→CaCl2(aq)+2H2O(l)+2NH3(g)

The ammonia is recycled back into the process to form ammoniated brine.

Calcium chloride is formed as a by-product of the Solvay process.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
According to the electrochemical series

K+ / K → -2.93V

Rb+ / Rb → -2.98V

Na+ / Na → -2.71V

Li+ / Li → -3.05V

Among the given alkali metals, sodium metal is the weakest reducing agent as its standard reduction potential is least negative which is ENa+/Na° = -2.71

So, the correct option is C.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Smaller the size of the cation, more the hydration of ion, hence the more values of the hydration enthalpies. Now, as the down the group the size increases so the hydration enthalpies decreases, hence as the increasing order of the size of the ions is as follows:
Be2+<Mg2+<Ca2+<Sr2+<Ba2+
and reverse of this order is true for the hydration enthalpies.
Be2+>Mg2+>Ca2+>Sr2+>Ba2+

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Cement is an important building material. It was first introduced in England in 1824 by Joseph Aspdin. It is also called Portland cement because it resembles with the natural limestone quarried in the Isle of Portland, England.

The raw materials for the manufacture of cement are limestone and clay.

When mixed with water, the setting of cement takes place to give a hard mass. This is due to the hydration of the molecules of the constituents and their rearrangement. The purpose of adding gypsum is only to slow down the process of setting of the cement so that it gets sufficiently hardened.

Hence,GypsumCaSO4·2H2O is used to enhance setting time in portland cement.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The colours of the flame are produced because of the movement of the electrons in the metal ions present in their salts. Calcium exhibits a brick-red colour. Strontium exhibits crimson colour. And the barium exhibits an apple green colour. 

K is an alkali metal. Potassium is colourless or white but when it is heated, it produces a violet flame due to the excitation of the electrons to the higher energy orbitals. Potassium burns with a light purple-coloured flame.

So, the correct option is C

options: Both Sr and Be elements are present in the ionic salt
Beis tetrahedrally coordinated in the ionic salt
The element Be is present in the cationic part of the ionic salt
The reaction is an example of acid-base neutralization reaction
solution:
BeOH2 has amphoteric nature. SrOH2 has basic nature. These two compounds undergo acid-base reaction to get salt,

BeOH2+SrOH2⟶Sr+2Be(OH)4-2

As Be is present in anionic part.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The size of alkali metals increases down the group, so volume also shows increment, and since volume is inversely proportional to the density but in the case Sr and Ba, increase in the volume is less compared to increasing mass.

So, down the group density decreases first and then increases.

Therefore, the correct order of density is as follows:

'Ca<Mg<Be<Sr

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Lithium has highest hydration enthalpy among
alkali metals due to its small size.
LiCl is soluble in pyridine because LiCl have
more covalent character.
Li does not form ethynide with ethyne.
Both Li and Mg reacts slowly with H 2 O

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Let's first balance the two given chemical equations:

Na2O+H2O⟶2NaOHX

In this equation, there are two NaOH molecules, so the total number of oxygen atoms in the products is 1.

Cl2O7+H2O⟶2HClO4Y

In this equation, there are two HClO4 molecules, so the total number of oxygen atoms in the products is 4.

So X has one O and Y has four O.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The atom with 55 protons is caesium(Cs). The electronic configuration of Cs is 1s22s22p63s23p64s23d104p65s24d105p66s1.

The mono positive ion means one electron is removed from outermost orbital. Hence, the sum of electrons in 1s, 2s, 3s, 4s, 5s orbitals are10.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
BeO  on reaction with HF in the presence of ammonia,  NH42BeF4 is formed which on heating forms BeF2  along with ammonium fluoride. 

BeO+2NH3+4HF→NH42BeF4+H2O

Compound A, is NH42BeF4

NH42BeF4→heatBeF2+2NH4F

Compound B is BeF2

In NH42BeF4 the oxidation state of Be is +2.

subject: Chemistry
Chemistry
solution:
(i) % Ionic character = 16(XA – XB) + 3.5 (XA – XB)2

X Rb = 0 · 8  ,     X Cl = 3 · 0 ,  XBe=2

For Rb and Cl the electronegativity difference is highest so having more ionic character. For Be and Cl, electronegativity difference is the lowest so having less ionic character

Covalent character in compound depends upon the charge and size of ions. As charge in cation increases, covalent character increases and as size of cation increases covalent character decreases. Charge factor dominate over size factor. Since Be2+ has higher charge and smallest size therefore, BeCl2 has maximum covalent character and least ionic character

solution:
The low solubility of LiF in water is due to its high lattice enthalpy.

The hydration enthalpies of alkali metal ions in aqueous solution decreases with increase in ionic sizes.

Li+>Na+>K+>Rb+>Cs+

Li+ has maximum degree of hydration and for this reason lithium salts are mostly hydrated, e.g., LiCl.2H2O

Hence, A is true and R is false.

Lithium metal is used to make useful alloys, for example with lead to make ‘white metal’ bearings for motor engines, with aluminium to make aircraft parts, and with magnesium to make armour plates. So, Alloy of Li and Mg is used to make armour plates and not aircraft plates.

Calcium plays important roles in neuromuscular function, interneuronal transmission and cell membrane integrity

solution:
Carbonates of alkaline earth metals are insoluble in water, and all the carbonates decompose on heating. Beryllium carbonate is unstable and can be kept only in the atmosphere of CO2. The thermal stability increases with increasing cationic size.
BeCO3<MgCO3<CaCO3<SrCO3<BaCO3
So the correct option is B.

The super oxide O2- ion is stable only in the presence of large cations K,Rb,Cs. K,Rb have IE1>400 kJ/mol from the table below. Ionisation enthalpy decreases down the group as metallic character increases.
 

Metal Li Na K Rb Cs
IE(KJ/mol) 520 496 419 403 376

subject: Chemistry
solution: Beryllium Halides are more covalent in nature, this is due to small size and high charge density of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math> ion. Hence it has high polarization power and more covalent character.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Metal halide on hydrolysis with water form corresponding hydroxides.
The basic strength of hydroxide increases as we move down in a group. This is because of the increase in size which results in decrease of ionization energy which weakens the strength of $\mathrm{M}-\mathrm{O}$ bonds in MOH and thus increases the basic strength.
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{Be}(\mathrm{OH})_2 \quad \mathrm{Mg}(\mathrm{OH})_2 \\
& \text { Amphoteric Weak base } \\
& \mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_2 \mathrm{Sr}(\mathrm{OH})_2 \mathrm{Ba}(\mathrm{OH})_2 \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
Hence, $\mathrm{Be}(\mathrm{OH})_2$ will have lowest $\mathrm{pH}$.

Li on reaction with excess air

4Li+O2→2Li2O

solution:
Slaked lime is Ca(OH)2, when it pass through carbon dioxide it form calcium carbonate, but in presence of excess carbon dioxide it forms calcium bicarbonate.

Slakedlime→CO2PassCaCO3↓→Prolonged passage of CO2CaHCO32

options: Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect.

Statement I and Statement II both are correct.
solution:
Statement-I is incorrect

BeOH2 dissolve in alkali due to it's amphoteric nature.

Statement-II is correct

Solubility of alkaline earth metal  hydroxide in water increases down the group due to rapid decreases in lattice energy as compared to hydration energy.

subject: Chemistry
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="bold">A</mi></math> is true but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="bold">R</mi></math> is false

Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="bold">A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="bold">R</mi></math> are true but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="bold">R</mi></math> is NOT the correct explanation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="bold">A</mi></math>
solution:
Sodium is indeed about 30 times more abundant than potassium in seawater. This is because sodium is more soluble in water than potassium, and is therefore more readily dissolved from rocks and minerals into seawater.

Potassium is bigger than sodium in size as atomic number of potassium is more than sodium and Potassium has one additional electron shell compared to sodium, which means that its atomic radius is larger.

So Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
